name: Jeffersonville Golf Club
about: Jeffersonville Golf Club is an 18-Hole, par 70 public course designed by Donald J. Ross, offering both on-course play and a state-of-the-art golf simulation facility. The venue includes six Trackman Golf Simulators under a facility called The Stables, which features a Bunker Bar. In addition to simulators, the club operates a full-service restaurant called The Burgess and offers banquet facilities for hosting various events, from weddings to corporate gatherings. The location is owned and operated by West Norriton Township and is consistently rated as one of the top 10 public golf courses in Pennsylvania.
why_golfers_like_it: State-of-the-art Trackman simulators offering access to over 300 global courses
On-course facility designed by legendary architect Donald J. Ross
Full-service Bunker Bar and The Burgess restaurant on-site
Advanced swing feedback with detailed Trackman data
Host for tournaments, corporate events, and special occasions

food_and_drink.notes: Full kitchen service at The Burgess Restaurant & Bar, serving steaks, seafood, salads, and more
Bunker Bar offers a variety of drinks, with special food and drink offerings available

simulator_hardware.notes: Six state-of-the-art Trackman simulators with detailed swing feedback

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: Yes, you'll need to bring your own golf clubs to use the simulators effectively.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: No, outside food and drinks are not permitted. The facility has The Burgess Restaurant and the Bunker Bar on-site for dining and beverage options.

faq.question: How long does it typically take to play 18 holes in the simulators?
faq.answer: Time to complete 18 holes can vary based on the number of players and pace of play, but typically ranges from 2-3 hours for a standard round.

faq.question: Is Jeffersonville Golf Club kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the venue appears to be kid-friendly with family-oriented amenities and events, though specific junior programs or age restrictions for simulator use weren't detailed.
